message:
fix(abi/resolv): reject known unterminated C strings and free filtered nodes

Harden the resolver ABI surface so unterminated malloc-backed C-string
inputs are rejected before any byte is dereferenced, and so filtered or
allocation-failed addrinfo chains do not leak.

resolv_abi.rs:

- opt_cstr now returns Result<Option<&CStr>, ()> and uses
  util::scan_c_string with known_remaining(ptr) to bound the scan over
  tracked malloc allocations. If the scan hits the end of the known
  region without seeing NUL, opt_cstr returns Err(()) instead of
  fabricating a CStr from unterminated bytes.
- getaddrinfo now matches on opt_cstr for both `node` and `service`,
  records a Bounds-stage outcome, observes runtime_policy, and returns
  EAI_FAIL or EAI_SERVICE on Err. Previous behavior would have fabricated
  a CStr view on potentially unterminated memory.
- gethostbyname / gethostbyname_r and getservbyname / getservbyport /
  getprotobyname adopt the same opt_cstr-based validation. Each error
  path sets the documented errno (EINVAL / NO_RECOVERY) and observes a
  bounds rejection in runtime_policy before returning the documented
  failure value.
- getservbyname / getprotobyname drop the now-redundant safety_mode
  binding (renamed to `_`) and unconditionally pass known_remaining as
  the bound to scan_c_string, since heals-disabled callers were already
  identical-shaped after this change.
- Introduce a free_addrinfo_node helper used in three new places:
  freeaddrinfo (replacing the inline raw free), the addrconfig family
  filter (which previously used Vec::retain and silently leaked
  filtered-out addrinfo allocations), and a new EAI_MEMORY error path
  that frees the partially-constructed chain when any builder slot
  produced a null pointer.
- The addrconfig filter is rewritten as an explicit per-element filter
  loop over a fresh Vec so dropped nodes are freed via
  free_addrinfo_node rather than retained-and-leaked.

resolv_abi_test.rs:

- Add a MallocCBuffer guard with an associated malloc_unterminated
  helper that allocates via crate::malloc_abi::malloc, copies an
  unterminated byte slice into it, and frees on drop. This produces a
  pointer that satisfies known_remaining bounds but has no terminator.
- New tests exercising the new rejection paths:
  - getaddrinfo_rejects_known_unterminated_node
  - getservbyname_rejects_known_unterminated_name
  - getservbyport_rejects_known_unterminated_proto
  - getprotobyname_rejects_known_unterminated_name
  Each asserts the call returns the documented failure (non-zero rc or
  null pointer) without mutating the result-out parameter.
